items.title: S&P 500 closes at record 7,609.78, Dow at 51,307.79
items.content: S&P 500 hit its 24th record close of 2026 at 7,609.78 on June 2. Dow followed on June 4 closing at 51,561.93. Nasdaq slightly pulled back to 26,830.96.

items.title: April PCE inflation rises to 3.8% YoY, core at 3.3%
items.content: Headline PCE price index rose 3.8% YoY in April, up from 3.5% in March. Core PCE rose 3.3% YoY. Monthly core PCE rose 0.2%, slightly below forecasts. Real PCE grew 0.1% while personal income fell slightly. Saving rate dropped to 2.6%.

items.title: US and Iran agree 60-day ceasefire extension, but tensions persist at Strait of Hormuz
items.content: A tentative 60-day ceasefire extension was reached on May 29, with plans for new talks on Iran's nuclear program. However, the US naval blockade remains in place. In early June, US forces shot down Iranian drones, and Iran continues to disrupt tanker crossings at the Strait of Hormuz. Oil prices remain elevated.

items.title: Hang Seng Index closes at 24,961.95, down 1.15% on tech/biotech pressure
items.content: HSI fell 1.15% on Friday. Down 4.78% over the past month but up 4.92% YoY. June rebalance scheduled for June 8 with three new additions. Hong Kong's Q1 GDP grew 5.9% YoY driven by trade and domestic demand.

items.title: China stock exchanges overhaul indices to include AI and semiconductor companies
items.content: Major Chinese exchanges rebalancing benchmarks mid-June to channel funds into domestic chip stocks. YMTC advancing IPO, revenue doubling YoY, capturing 13% of global NAND market. CXMT receives STAR Market IPO approval. Huawei holds ~50% of China's AI chip market.

items.title: Best Buy Q1 FY27 beats estimates: revenue $8.94B, EPS $1.31
items.content: Revenue $8.94B (+1.9% YoY), beating $8.82B consensus. Comparable sales +2.0%. EPS $1.31 vs $1.23 consensus. Operating margin improved to 4.1% from 2.5%. Reiterated full-year guidance $6.30-$6.60 EPS.

items.title: Snowflake Q1 FY27 revenue $1.39B, +33.5% YoY, beats consensus
items.content: Product revenue grew 30% YoY to $1.23B in Q4 FY26. Q1 FY27 revenue $1.39B (+33.5% YoY), EPS $0.39 vs $0.32 consensus. Net loss narrowed to $295.57M. Remaining performance obligations $9.77B (+42% YoY).

items.title: Dollar Tree Q1 FY26 beats: revenue $4.98B, adj. EPS $1.74, raises guidance
items.content: Revenue $4.98B (+7.2% YoY). Comparable sales +3.5% driven by 4.5% higher average ticket. Adjusted EPS $1.74 (+38% YoY), beating $1.53 consensus. Raised full-year adjusted EPS guidance to $6.70-$7.10.

items.title: US May jobs report: expected 95K additions, unemployment at 4.3%
items.content: May non-farm payrolls data released June 5. Market expected 95,000 jobs added with unemployment rate at 4.3%. This follows April's data showing a cooling labor market.

items.title: Hong Kong Q1 GDP grows 5.9% YoY, unemployment steady at 3.7%
items.content: HK economy expanded 5.9% YoY in Q1 2026, driven by external trade and domestic demand. Inflation steady at 1.7% in April. Full-year GDP forecast 2.5%-3.5%. Unemployment remained at 3.7%.

items.title: US forces shoot down Iranian drones, strike radar sites after ceasefire violations
items.content: US military shot down Iranian drones and missiles in early June, followed by retaliatory strikes on Iranian radar sites. Iran accuses US of ceasefire violations; US cites self-defense. Strait of Hormuz disruptions continue to pressure global energy markets.

items.title: Central bank meetings ahead: ECB June 10-11, FOMC June 16-17, BOJ June 15-16
items.content: Key June central bank meetings scheduled. ECB meets June 10-11, FOMC June 16-17, BOJ June 15-16. Markets watching for rate decisions amid sticky inflation (US PCE 3.8%) and geopolitical tensions.
